heading: CENSUS
label: 13 CENSUS
notes: This title was enacted by ,act Aug. 31, 1954, ch. 115868 Stat. 1012
Amendments1990—, , , added item for chapter 10.Pub. L. 101–533, § 5(b)(1)104 Stat. 2348Nov. 7, 19901962—, , , added item for chapter 9.Pub. L. 87–826, § 176 Stat. 951Oct. 15, 1962

Positive Law; CitationThis title has been made positive law by , , which provided in part “That title 13 of the United States Code, entitled ‘Census’, is revised, codified, and enacted into law, and may be cited as ‘Title 13, United States Code, section—’ ”.act Aug. 31, 1954, ch. 1158, § 168 Stat. 1012

References to Census Office, , provided that:Act Aug. 31, 1954, ch. 1158, § 368 Stat. 1025“Whenever reference is made in any other law or in any regulation or order to the Census Office, such reference shall be held and considered to mean the Bureau of the Census referred to in , United States Code, as set out in section 1 of this Act. This section shall not be construed as affecting historical references to the Census Office which could have no present or future application to the Bureau of the Census.”section 2 of Title 13

Separability, , provided that:Act Aug. 31, 1954, ch. 1158, § 468 Stat. 1025“If any part of Title 13, United States Code, as set in section 1 of this Act, is held invalid, the remainder of such title shall not be affected thereby.”

Legislative Construction, , provided that:Act Aug. 31, 1954, ch. 1158, § 568 Stat. 1025“No inference of a legislative construction is to be drawn by reason of the chapter in Title 13, United States Code, as set out in section 1 of this Act, in which any section is placed, nor by reason of the captions or catchlines used in such title.”
Effective Date, , provided that:Act Aug. 31, 1954, ch. 1158, § 668 Stat. 1025“The provisions of this Act shall take effect on .”January 1, 1955

Repeals, , provided that:Act Aug. 31, 1954, ch. 1158, § 768 Stat. 1025“The sections of the Acts, and the Acts or parts of Acts, enumerated in the following schedule, are hereby repealed. Any rights or liabilities now existing under such statutes or parts thereof, and any proceedings instituted under, or growing out of, any of such statutes or parts thereof, shall not be affected by this repeal.”
